title: Weather news, June 28: 80 km wind, thunderstorm and…, IMD issues heavy rainfall warning for these 19 states, Delhi, Noida, Gurugram, Bihar, HP, Uttarakhand, J-K to…; Check full IMD forecast here - news24online.com

Weather news, June 28: 80 km wind, thunderstorm and…, IMD issues heavy rainfall warning for these 19 states, Delhi, Noida, Gurugram, Bihar, HP, Uttarakhand, J-K to…; Check full IMD forecast here
Across India, while several states continue to reel under intense heatwave conditions, the southwest monsoon is advancing rapidly. The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued a thunderstorm and heavy rainfall alert for 19 states, warning of strong winds with speeds ranging from 60 to 80 kmph. Residents are advised to check the latest weather updates before stepping outdoors. According to the IMD's forecast, heavy to very heavy rainfall (7–20 cm) is likely over Northeast India and Sub-Himalayan West Bengal and Sikkim during the week. Extremely heavy rainfall is expected at isolated places over Sub-Himalayan West Bengal and Sikkim from June 27 to 29, while Assam and Meghalaya may witness extremely heavy rainfall on June 28, 2026. Meanwhile, heatwave to severe heatwave conditions are likely to persist over Uttar Pradesh for the next two days, creating a sharp contrast in weather conditions across different parts of the country.

Meanwhile, heatwave to severe heatwave conditions are likely to persist over Uttar Pradesh for the next two days, creating a sharp contrast in weather conditions across different parts of the country.
Delhi Weather Today
Delhi is expected to witness partly cloudy skies on June 28. Thunderstorms and strong winds are likely from the afternoon to evening, with the maximum temperature around 31°C. Light rain with thunderstorms is expected on June 29, while similar conditions may continue on June 30. Wind speeds could reach 40–60 kmph.
Uttar Pradesh Weather Today
The IMD has issued a heatwave alert for most districts of Uttar Pradesh, including Lucknow, Varanasi, Prayagraj, Gorakhpur, Ayodhya, and Sitapur. No rainfall is expected today. However, conditions will become favorable for the southwest monsoon over the next 3–4 days. Rainfall is likely to increase across western Uttar Pradesh from June 29, which may bring a drop of up to 9°C in temperatures.
Bihar Weather Today
Monsoon activity will gradually strengthen across Bihar. Scattered rainfall is expected initially, while widespread rain is likely between June 29 and July 1. Heavy to extremely heavy rainfall is expected in northeastern Bihar, while southwestern parts may continue to face heatwave conditions for the next two days. Patna may receive light rain between June 28 and 30, while Gaya is likely to see showers on June 28 and 29. Heavy rain is expected today in Katihar, Purnia, and Bhagalpur.
Himachal Pradesh & Uttarakhand Weather
Himachal Pradesh is likely to receive light rain on June 28, accompanied by strong winds of 30–40 kmph. Rainfall activity is expected to intensify between July 1 and 3, with heavy rain likely in several places, including Shimla, Mandi, Kullu, Kangra, and Solan. In Uttarakhand, light to heavy rain is expected between June 28 and 30 in Dehradun, Nainital, Haridwar, Pauri Garhwal, Chamoli, and Rudraprayag. There is also a risk of landslides in Nainital.
Jharkhand & Jammu and Kashmir Weather
Jharkhand is likely to witness light to heavy rain with thunderstorms in Ranchi, Jamshedpur, Bokaro, Hazaribagh, and nearby districts until July 1. Strong winds of 40–50 kmph are also expected, with widespread rainfall likely from June 29 onwards. In Jammu and Kashmir, light to heavy rain is forecast in districts including Jammu, Srinagar, Anantnag, Baramulla, and Kupwara through July 3. Rainfall is expected to become more widespread from June 30, along with thunderstorms and gusty winds reaching 40–50 kmph.
